Task: Read the current backend blueprint before coding anything
Methods or classes to create first: Lock final list of roles, endpoints, token flow, OTP flow, reset flow, admin APIs, OAuth providers
Acceptance or exit check: You have one fixed backend scope and do not redesign mid-build

Task: Create the Spring Boot backend app and package structure
Methods or classes to create first: Add Spring Boot Web, Security, Data JPA, Validation, Mail, Thymeleaf, OAuth2 Client, Redis, PostgreSQL, JWT, Lombok, MapStruct, Test dependencies; AuthApplication.main()
Acceptance or exit check: `mvn -q -DskipTests compile` can resolve dependencies and the app starts

Task: Create backend config skeleton and environment placeholders
Methods or classes to create first: Create properties for server, datasource, redis, mail, jwt, frontend URLs, cookie settings, seed admin, rate limits, OAuth2 providers, OTP/reset expiry
Acceptance or exit check: App can boot locally once real secrets are supplied

Task: Create centralized API path constants
Acceptance or exit check: All controllers can share one versioned base path

Task: Register password encoder bean
Acceptance or exit check: BCrypt encoder is injectable anywhere passwords are created or checked

Task: Create explicit CORS config for frontend origins
Acceptance or exit check: Browser requests with credentials work only for trusted origins

Task: Enable stable Page JSON serialization
Acceptance or exit check: Admin user pagination responses serialize cleanly

Task: Create role enum and role entity
Methods or classes to create first: RoleName enum with ROLE_USER and ROLE_ADMIN; Role entity
Acceptance or exit check: JPA creates `roles` table and role enum is ready for RBAC

Task: Create the user aggregate with auth lifecycle fields
Methods or classes to create first: User fields: id, name, email, password, enabled, verificationOtp, otpExpiry, resetToken, resetTokenExpiry, refreshToken, refreshTokenExpiry, failedLoginAttempts, accountLockedUntil, failedOtpAttempts, otpLockedUntil, authProvider, authProviderUserId, createdAt, updatedAt, roles; onCreate(); onUpdate()
Acceptance or exit check: User table can represent local auth, OAuth, OTP, reset, refresh, and lockout state in one record

Task: Create repositories for users and roles
Methods or classes to create first: findByName(); findByEmailIgnoreCase(); existsByEmailIgnoreCase(); findByResetToken(); findByRefreshToken(); findByAuthProviderAndAuthProviderUserId(); countByEnabledTrue()
Acceptance or exit check: All required DB lookups for auth and admin screens are available

Task: Create shared IST timestamp helper
Acceptance or exit check: Timestamps are consistent across entities, dashboards, and DTOs

Task: Create request payload classes first
Methods or classes to create first: Add validation annotations for name, email, password, token, OTP
Acceptance or exit check: Request validation fails early before service logic runs

Task: Create response and dashboard DTOs
Methods or classes to create first: MessageResponse; AuthResponse; AuthTokens record; UserDto; UserDashboardDto; AdminDashboardDto
Acceptance or exit check: API contracts are fixed before controllers are written

Task: Create MapStruct mapper between entities and DTOs
Methods or classes to create first: toEntity(); toDto(); toDtoList(); toUserDashboardDto(); map(Role); map(LocalDateTime); resolveLoginSource()
Acceptance or exit check: Entity-to-response mapping is centralized and repeatable

Task: Create role service abstraction and implementation
Acceptance or exit check: Roles can be seeded or assigned without controller code touching repositories

Task: Create user service abstraction and implementation
Methods or classes to create first: getUserByEmail(); findByEmail(); existsByEmail(); save(); findByResetToken(); findByRefreshToken(); findByAuthProviderAndAuthProviderUserId(); normalizeEmail()
Acceptance or exit check: User access, normalization, and save behavior are centralized

Task: Create startup seeding for roles and optional admin account
Acceptance or exit check: App can create missing roles and optional admin user on startup

Feature: Create custom exceptions and one global REST handler
Task: backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/UserAlreadyExistsException.java; backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/ResourceNotFoundException.java; backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/TokenValidationException.java; backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/AccountLockedException.java; backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/RateLimitExceededException.java; backend/src/main/java/com/auth/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java
Files to create or update: handleValidationExceptions(); handleBadCredentialsException(); handleUserAlreadyExistsException(); handleResourceNotFoundException(); handleTokenValidationException(); handleIllegalArgumentException(); handleException()
Depends on: API errors return predictable JSON instead of stack traces

Task: Create reusable password rules before register and reset
Acceptance or exit check: Registration and password updates share one password policy

Task: Create hash service for OTP, reset token, and refresh token storage
Acceptance or exit check: Raw sensitive tokens are never stored in the database

Task: Create secure generators for OTP and reset tokens
Acceptance or exit check: You can issue 6-digit OTPs and high-entropy reset tokens

Task: Create Spring Security user loader
Acceptance or exit check: Spring Security can authenticate existing users from the DB

Task: Create access-token generator and validator
Methods or classes to create first: generateToken(); generateTokenFromEmail(); getEmailFromToken(); validateToken(); getSigningKey(); decodeSecret()
Acceptance or exit check: You can mint and validate bearer access tokens

Task: Create request filter that reads bearer token and fills SecurityContext
Acceptance or exit check: Protected requests authenticate from `Authorization: Bearer ...`

Task: Create HTTP-only refresh cookie builder
Acceptance or exit check: Refresh token cookie settings are centralized and reusable

Task: Wire filter chain, RBAC, DAO auth provider, and OAuth placeholders
Acceptance or exit check: Public and protected routes are enforced correctly

Task: Create the auth service interface before the implementation
Methods or classes to create first: register(); verifyOtp(); login(); resetPassword(); updatePassword(); resendOtp(); changePassword()
Acceptance or exit check: You have a single contract for all account lifecycle operations

Task: Create mail service with OTP delivery first
Methods or classes to create first: sendOtpEmail(String toEmail, String otp); sendOtpEmail(String toEmail, String recipientName, String otp); sendHtmlEmail(); resolveRecipientName()
Acceptance or exit check: Registration can send OTP email without blocking the rest of the architecture

Task: Create service for access-token issuance and refresh rotation
Methods or classes to create first: issueTokens(); refreshTokens(); revokeRefreshToken(); buildAuthResponse(); generateRefreshToken(); hasRefreshTokenExpired(); clearStoredRefreshToken()
Acceptance or exit check: Login and refresh can issue JWT + persisted hashed refresh token

Task: Implement register flow first
Acceptance or exit check: New users are saved disabled, password is encoded, OTP is hashed, ROLE_USER is assigned

Task: Implement verify-OTP next
Methods or classes to create first: verifyOtp(); getExistingUserByEmail(); ensureEmailNotYetVerified(); ensureOtpMatches(); ensureTokenNotExpired(); sendWelcomeEmailSafely()
Acceptance or exit check: Correct OTP enables account, clears OTP state, and sends welcome email

Task: Implement email-password login after register + verify work
Acceptance or exit check: Verified users can authenticate and receive access token + refresh token

Task: Create public auth endpoints for register, verify, and login
Acceptance or exit check: Frontend or Postman can hit the first three auth APIs successfully

Task: Add refresh and logout endpoints once token rotation exists
Acceptance or exit check: Refresh rotates refresh token and logout clears stored token + cookie

Task: Expand email service for all remaining auth notifications
Methods or classes to create first: sendPasswordResetEmail(); sendWelcomeEmail(); sendPasswordChangedConfirmationEmail(); sendAccountLockedAlertEmail(); buildPasswordResetHtmlBodyFromTemplate(); buildWelcomeHtmlBodyFromTemplate(); buildPasswordChangedHtmlBodyFromTemplate(); buildAccountLockedHtmlBodyFromTemplate()
Acceptance or exit check: Every account lifecycle event has an email method and HTML template

Task: Implement reset-password request flow
Acceptance or exit check: Known users get reset link, unknown users still receive generic success response

Task: Implement password update via reset token
Acceptance or exit check: Token must be valid and not expired; password updates and reset token is cleared

Task: Implement resend-OTP flow after verification logic exists
Acceptance or exit check: Unverified users can receive a fresh OTP and verified users cannot

Task: Implement change-password for logged-in users
Acceptance or exit check: Current password is checked and new password follows the policy

Task: Create user-facing service for dashboard and profile
Acceptance or exit check: Authenticated users can fetch a dashboard payload and profile DTO

Task: Create protected user endpoints
Acceptance or exit check: ROLE_USER and ROLE_ADMIN can access profile/dashboard and change password

Task: Create admin dashboard and user listing service
Methods or classes to create first: getDashboard(); getUsers(); buildUserSpecification(); normalizeRoleName(); resolveSortField(); normalizePage(); normalizePageSize(); resolveSortDirection(); buildSearchSpecification(); andSpecification(); isAllowedSortField()
Acceptance or exit check: Admin metrics and paginated filtered user listing work

Task: Create protected admin endpoints
Acceptance or exit check: ROLE_ADMIN can access dashboard and filtered user list endpoints

Task: Create Redis-backed rate-limit helper
Acceptance or exit check: Request counters with TTL work and fail open if Redis is unavailable

Task: Create auth abuse orchestration service after happy path auth is stable
Methods or classes to create first: guardLoginAttempt(); recordFailedLogin(); clearLoginFailures(); guardOtpVerification(); recordFailedOtp(); clearOtpFailures(); guardResendOtp(); guardResetPassword(); enforce(); assertLoginNotLocked(); assertOtpNotLocked(); resolveClientIp(); applyFailedLoginAttempt(); sendAccountLockEmailSafely()
Acceptance or exit check: Login, OTP, resend, and reset flows have per-IP and per-email protection plus account locks

Task: Update auth flows and error handler to expose lock and throttle behavior
Methods or classes to create first: Call guardLoginAttempt(); recordFailedLogin(); clearLoginFailures(); guardOtpVerification(); recordFailedOtp(); clearOtpFailures(); guardResendOtp(); guardResetPassword(); add handleAccountLockedException(); handleRateLimitExceededException()
Acceptance or exit check: Clients receive 423 or 429 with `Retry-After` and the auth flows enforce protection

Task: Create local user provisioning for Google, GitHub, Apple, and LinkedIn
Methods or classes to create first: loadOrCreateUser(); createNewOAuthUser(); updateExistingUserIfNeeded(); extractEmail(); extractDisplayName(); normalizeProvider(); extractProviderUserId(); buildProviderDerivedEmail()
Acceptance or exit check: OAuth logins can create or link local users safely

Task: Create handlers for OAuth callback outcomes
Acceptance or exit check: Successful OAuth login issues refresh cookie and redirects; failures redirect with an error

Task: Create LinkedIn authorization request resolver
Methods or classes to create first: resolve(HttpServletRequest); resolve(HttpServletRequest, String); customizeForLinkedIn(); isLinkedInRequest(); removeNonce()
Acceptance or exit check: LinkedIn OIDC flow works without nonce mismatch issues

Task: Finish OAuth2 registrations and security wiring
Methods or classes to create first: Add `oauth2Login()` wiring, success handler, failure handler, LinkedIn resolver, and provider registration properties
Acceptance or exit check: `/oauth2/authorization/{provider}` starts provider auth and callback completes local sign-in

Task: Write unit tests for controller and core auth/token flows
Methods or classes to create first: Test refresh candidate resolution, logout cookie clearing, register, login failure, reset generic response, token validation, refresh expiry handling
Acceptance or exit check: High-risk auth flows are covered before frontend integration

Task: Write tests for security user loading, user service, admin service, and OAuth provisioning
Methods or classes to create first: Test email normalization, authority mapping, repository-driven user lookup, paging normalization, invalid role filter, OAuth fallback emails, provider linking
Acceptance or exit check: Security, admin, and OAuth edge cases are covered

Task: Create backend container and local stack orchestration
Methods or classes to create first: Wire postgres, redis, app, optional admin tools, environment variables, health ordering
Acceptance or exit check: Backend can run reproducibly with PostgreSQL and Redis

Task: Run compile and test before connecting frontend
Methods or classes to create first: Run `mvn -q -DskipTests compile`; run `mvn -q test`; smoke: register -> verify OTP -> login -> refresh -> logout -> reset password -> user profile -> admin users
Acceptance or exit check: Build is green and major backend flows work end to end
